timingInterpretationPolicy.guidance: When timingInterpretation.selectedTiming.scopeClass is narrow-hot-path, deltaPercent remains a phase-specific diagnostic. Claimability evaluates timingInterpretation.headlineProcessWall.deltaPercent when that end-to-end metric is available.

workloads.name: dispatch fallback contract
workloads.description: Directional dispatch benchmark exercising the builtin fallback dispatch command path.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Directional-only dispatch contract. This benchmark is intentionally non-claimable until a Dawn-side comparable dispatch-likelihood lane is added.

timingNormalization.note: Directional dispatch contract only: normalize per fallback dispatch operation.

comparabilityCandidate.notes: Promotable once a Dawn compute-dispatch parity contract is established.

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing measures the comparable operation scope. headlineProcessWall adds process startup and harness overhead for the timed command.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the end-to-end ranking view for one comparable workload unit.

reasons: workload is marked non-comparable by workload contract
left side has no successful execution samples (executionSuccessCount=0)

workloads.name: dispatch grid contract
workloads.description: Directional dispatch contract exercising non-trivial 3D dispatch grid shapes through fallback dispatch command path.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Directional-only dispatch protocol contract. This benchmark is intentionally non-claimable until a Dawn compute-dispatch parity lane is added.

timingNormalization.note: Directional dispatch grid contract only: normalize across both-dispatch-sequence workload steps.

comparabilityCandidate.notes: Promotable once a Dawn multi-shape dispatch parity contract is established.

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing measures the comparable operation scope. headlineProcessWall adds process startup and harness overhead for the timed command.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the end-to-end ranking view for one comparable workload unit.

reasons: workload is marked non-comparable by workload contract
workload is marked non-comparable by workload contract
left side has no successful execution samples (executionSuccessCount=0)
workload is marked non-comparable by workload contract
